provenance.notes: Processed 3 quotes from 4 verse files. Source: HistoricalChristianFaith/Commentaries-Database. License: PUBLIC DOMAIN.

data.author: Pseudo-Tertullian
data.quote:
As corrector of an inert People
That emulator [of David] Hezekiah arose;
He restored the Law to a sinful, forgetful people
All God’s mandates of old, he first
Commanded the people to observe,
Who ended war by his prayers,
Not by steel’s point: he, dying, had a grant
Of years and times of life made to his tears:
Deservedly such honor his career obtained.
data.source_title: FIVE BOOKS IN REPLY TO MARCION 3.176-183

data.author: Pseudo-Tertullian
data.quote:
With zeal immense, Josiah,
Himself a prince, acted in such a way
As no one before or after him had ever done!—Idols he
Dethroned; destroyed unhallowed temples; burned
With fire priests on their altars; all the bones
Of false prophets were dug up; the altars burned,
The carcasses to be consumed did serve
For fuel!
data.source_title: FIVE BOOKS IN REPLY TO MARCION 3.184-191

data.author: Pseudo-Tertullian
data.quote: For of Judaism’s heretics I am silent - Dositheus the Samaritan, I mean, who was the first who had the hardihood to repudiate the prophets, on the ground that they had not spoken under inspiration of the Holy Spirit. Of the Sadducees I am silent, who, springing from the root of this error, had the hardihood to adjoin to this heresy the denial likewise of the resurrection of the flesh.
